architecture_fit.note: Minimal attention-only softmax(QK^T/√d) on GPU solves the task perfectly, but the scored fn is identical to the generator so fidelity is tautological; merit is in the surrounding analysis.

baseline_comparison.note: Four well-motivated strawmen (no_softmax, linear_taylor, wrong_temp, uniform) all measured under identical conditions and shown losing on the KL bar chart.

faithfulness.note: Adds necessity/sufficiency causal tests, but separation is negligible in canonical/orthogonal/cos conditions (top≈random); only uniform shows a clear gap, so the causal evidence is weak where it matters.

operating_range.note: Logit scale swept 0.1×→10× (2 orders) locating the linear_taylor breaking point; softmax stays flat at 1.0 trivially since it equals the generator at every scale.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set analytically with no training — the softmax circuit and all alternatives are written out by hand on the GPU.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is legible: side-by-side heatmaps, KL bars, operating-range lines, causal bars each make a distinct point, though the causal panels visually show little separation.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' ties each chart to a specific testable claim (eyeball recovery, win-vs-alternatives, located breaking point, causal closure) with correct axes and baselines.

notes: A thoughtful, self-aware iteration that reframes a tautological goal (true attribution IS softmax(QK^T/√d)) into a mechanism-selection problem, with strong multi-strawman baselines, a real operating-range sweep that locates where the linear surrogate breaks, and a fully hand-built circuit. The main weakness is faithfulness: the added necessity/sufficiency causal tests show almost no top-vs-random separation in the canonical and aligned conditions, so they don't strongly establish that the flagged keys are the ones actually used. Mean of scored items ≈4.3 → good.
